item.description: The Trump administration and Iran both say progress is being made to end the war but there’s no deal yet. President Donald Trump says he’s told his representatives “not to rush into a deal,” while an Iran spokesman said, “No one can claim an agreement is about to be signed imminently.” The key terms at issue are Iran’s nuclear program and the fate of nearly 1,000lbs of highly enriched uranium, and control over the Strait of Hormuz. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Trump Says He Told Negotiators 'Not to Rush Into' a Deal With Iran
media:content.media:description: The Trump administration and Iran both say progress is being made to end the war but there’s no deal yet. President Donald Trump says he’s told his representatives “not to rush into a deal,” while an Iran spokesman said, “No one can claim an agreement is about to be signed imminently.” The key terms at issue are Iran’s nuclear program and the fate of nearly 1,000lbs of highly enriched uranium, and control over the Strait of Hormuz. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

item.title: Trump Says He Told Negotiators 'Not to Rush Into' a Deal With Iran

item.description: In South Korea, an American company is mining for rare minerals used to make weapons and ammunition. Trade tensions with China have strained access to the mineral, tungsten, and the war with Iran is forcing the U.S. to find even more of it. NBC’s Janis Mackey Frayer reports from South Korea.

media:content.media:title: Inside the South Korean mines that will provide U.S. with key metal for Iran War
media:content.media:description: In South Korea, an American company is mining for rare minerals used to make weapons and ammunition. Trade tensions with China have strained access to the mineral, tungsten, and the war with Iran is forcing the U.S. to find even more of it. NBC’s Janis Mackey Frayer reports from South Korea.

item.title: Inside the South Korean mines that will provide U.S. with key metal for Iran War

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Steve Patterson
Shanshan Dong
Aria Bendix
item.description: A potential crack may be releasing pressure inside a toxic chemical tank in Garden Grove, officials said Sunday, a development they hope could help avert a devastating vapor explosion.

media:content.media:text: Steam and mist rising off of industrial tanks.
media:content.media:description: An aerial of water being sprayed on large storage tanks at the GKN Aerospace facility in Garden Grove, Calif. May 24, 2026.

media:content.media:text: A drone photo of an industrial yard surrounded by train tracks.
media:content.media:description: The company said Sunday it was monitoring the condition of the “affected material” and crews are working “around the clock to mitigate the risk of a leak.”

media:content.media:text: Two red fire trucks parked in the middle of a street.
media:content.media:description: Officials are concerned the tank at GKN Aerospace could rupture and release toxic material into waterways and vapors into the air.

media:content.media:text: An eight lane highway devoid of any traffic in California, seen from the air.
media:content.media:description: Empty streets in Garden Grove, Calif., on Sunday. Shelters in Orange County are filling up with frantic locals as 50,000 people are under evacuation orders.

media:content.media:text: People stand in a line in a gymnasium.
media:content.media:description: Evacuees at a temporary shelter in Garden Grove on Friday.

item.title: Risk of devastating explosion has been ‘eliminated’ in California toxic chemical tank incident, officials say

item.dc:creator: Anagilmara Vílchez
Ronny Rojas
item.description: A Noticias Telemundo investigation documented a marked increase in cases of people posing as federal agents to rob, intimidate and even injure and rape immigrants.

media:content.media:text: A Mexican immigrant in Greensboro, N.C., says his life has been forever changed following an armed robbery by men posing as ICE agents. He has turned to his faith for protection and installed additional locks on his door.
Bloodstains in one of the bedrooms after the assault by people impersonating ICE officers in Greensboro, N.C.
Clau's injuries required ten stitches and four staples.
Joshua Warner
Hank Glembin
After an incident with a fake ICE agent, this young Mexican immigrant says he has been struggling with anxiety attacks and insomnia.
Latrance Battle is arrested in Florida.
ICE Detains Immigrants Inside New York City Courthouses
Steven Donnovan in a screenshot from the body-worn camera footage obtained by Noticias Telemundo.
Jose Juan Lopez
Federal agents conduct immigration enforcement operations on Feb. 5, 2026, in Minneapolis.

item.title: Crimes involving fake ICE agents are on the rise and becoming ‘much more aggressive and violent’

item.description: Pope Leo XIV is calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence in his newly released manifesto on safeguarding humanity in an era of increasing reliance on AI. In the highly anticipated document that’s been in the works for more than a year, the Pope urges, “Let’s not fear artificial intelligence but constantly keep the question of the human in play. We cannot be careless with our most powerful technical instruments.”

media:content.media:title: Pope Leo XIV Warns of Risks of AI, Calls for Robust Regulation
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV is calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence in his newly released manifesto on safeguarding humanity in an era of increasing reliance on AI. In the highly anticipated document that’s been in the works for more than a year, the Pope urges, “Let’s not fear artificial intelligence but constantly keep the question of the human in play. We cannot be careless with our most powerful technical instruments.”

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Warns of Risks of AI, Calls for Robust Regulation

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Onstage and on the sidelines of the recent Jewish Democratic Council of America Leadership Summit in Washington, prominent pro-Israel Democrats tiptoed into one of the biggest debates roiling their party

media:content.media:text: Schumer and Jeffries News Conference
media:content.media:description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, left, and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries both spoke at the recent Jewish Democratic Council of America Leadership Summit.

item.title: Jewish Democrats grapple with a changing party and Israel’s entrenched leadership

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: Dead, emaciated gray whales have been washing up on Washington state shores at a shocking pace over the last few months.

media:content.media:text: Gray whale washed ashore in Oak Harbor, WA
media:content.media:description: A dead gray whale in Oak Harbor, Wash., on May 18.

media:content.media:text: Tourists watch from a panga as a gray whale surfaces and spouts
media:content.media:description: A dead gray whale in Oak Harbor, Wash., on May 18.

item.title: 3 months, 21 dead gray whales: Why so many carcasses are washing up on Pacific shores

item.dc:creator: Isabella Colletta
item.description: Allegations of rape and sexual abuse on the British version of the reality show “Married at First Sight” have rattled the industry and led to threats of tighter regulation

media:content.media:text: The bride puts the wedding ring on the groom's finger.
media:content.media:description: The allegations have led to the hit show's being dropped by its biggest sponsor.

media:content.media:text: Investigation Into Rape Allegations Following Channel 4's 'Married at First Sight' Programme
media:content.media:description: The allegations have led to the hit show's being dropped by its biggest sponsor.

item.title: Allegations of rape on ‘Married at First Sight UK’ rattle the reality-TV industry

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Brooklinen, Ninja, Levoit, EltaMD and Apple.

media:content.media:credit: Amazon; Kara Birnbaum / NBC News; Brooklinen

media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Elyse Perlmutter-Gumbiner
Jane C. Timm
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to go to Walter Reed National Military Medical Center on Tuesday for a medical and dental check-up, according to a White House official

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump in the Oval Office on May 21, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump in the Oval Office on May 21.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Hosts South Korean President Lee Jae Myung At White House
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump in the Oval Office on May 21.

item.title: Trump to visit Walter Reed for the third check-up of his second term

item.dc:creator: Janis Mackey Frayer
Dawn Liu
Jennifer Jett
item.description: Chinese tech giant Huawei said Monday that it had achieved a breakthrough that would allow it to make cutting-edge chips within five years, touting the news as a significant milestone in Beijing’s effort to circumvent U.S. technology restrictions.

media:content.media:text: MWC 2025 In Shanghai
media:content.media:description: Huawei stand at the Shanghai New International Expo Centre, China, in 2025.

media:content.media:text: CHINA-US-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: Huawei stand at the Shanghai New International Expo Centre, China, in 2025.

item.title: China’s Huawei touts chip design breakthrough in bid to defy U.S. sanctions

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has called for AI advances to be slowed down while blaming the technology for fueling conflict around the world.

media:content.media:text: Vatican Pool Images 2026
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV presides over Mass for the Solemnity of Pentecost at St. Peter's Basilica on May 24, 2026 in Vatican City, Vatican.

media:content.media:text: VATICAN-ENCYCLICAL-MAGNIFICA HUMANITAS-AI-POPE
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo at the presentation of his first encyclical at the Vatican on Monday.

item.title: Pope Leo warns AI is fueling conflict, urges world to ‘slow’ advances

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
item.description: Iran warned Monday that an agreement to end the war launched by the United States and Israel was not imminent, after President Donald Trump raised and then lowered expectations that a deal may be close

media:content.media:text: Iranians Gather To Commemorate War Dead
media:content.media:description: People hold portraits of the dead at the Imam Khomeini Mosalla Mosque to commemorate those killed in the current US-Israeli war as well as previous wars on May 24, 2026 in Tehran, Iran.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-INDIA-US-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and his wife, Jeanette Rubio, at the Taj Mahal in Agra, India, on Monday.

media:content.media:text: INDIA-US-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: Rubio’s four-day trip includes talks with members from the Indo-Pacific alliance known as the Quad.

media:content.media:text: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz are visible near the beach of Bandar Abbas
media:content.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz are visible near the beach of Bandar Abbas, Iran, on Friday.

item.title: Iran says no deal ‘imminent’ despite progress in talks with U.S.

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar

item.dc:creator: Janis Mackey Frayer
Stella Kim
Jennifer Jett
item.description: The United States is burning through weapons stockpiles in Iran, and replacing them will require large amounts of tungsten, a powerful metal whose supply is dominated by China.

media:content.media:text: Almonty Industries' Sangdong tungsten mine in South Korea.
media:content.media:description: Almonty Industries' Sangdong tungsten mine in South Korea.

media:content.media:text: A mining district in China’s Inner Mongolia region in October.
media:content.media:description: A mining district in China’s Inner Mongolia region in October.

media:content.media:text: Almonty Industries’ Sangdong tungsten mine in South Korea.
media:content.media:description: Almonty Industries’ Sangdong tungsten mine in South Korea.

media:content.media:text: Tungsten cut out isolated on white background
media:content.media:description: Tungsten.

media:content.media:text: Almonty says it will process about 1.2 million tons of tungsten ore a year at the South Korean mine.
media:content.media:description: Almonty says it will process about 1.2 million tons of tungsten ore a year at the South Korean mine.

item.title: China controls a metal that’s key for the Iran war, sending the U.S. on a global hunt for more

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Steve Patterson
Shanshan Dong
Aria Bendix
item.description: A potential crack may be releasing pressure inside a toxic chemical tank in Garden Grove, officials said Sunday, a development they hope could help avert a devastating vapor explosion.

media:content.media:description: The company said Sunday it was monitoring the condition of the “affected material” and crews are working “around the clock to mitigate the risk of a leak.”

media:content.media:text: Two red fire trucks parked in the middle of a street.
media:content.media:description: Officials are concerned the tank at GKN Aerospace could rupture and release toxic material into waterways and vapors into the air.

media:content.media:text: An eight lane highway devoid of any traffic in California, seen from the air.

media:content.media:text: People stand in a line in a gymnasium.

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Buckle up: The Mercedes intra-team battle for the Formula 1 drivers’ world championship just kicked into high gear

media:content.media:text: Kimi Antonelli pumps a fist into the air while wearing a race helmet.
media:content.media:description: Race winner Kimi Antonelli of Italy celebrates following the F1 Grand Prix of Canada in Montreal on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Two cars on a racetrack outside
media:content.media:description: Race winner Kimi Antonelli of Italy celebrates following the F1 Grand Prix of Canada in Montreal on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: A blurred photo of a race car passing on the track.
media:content.media:description: Race winner Kimi Antonelli of Italy celebrates following the F1 Grand Prix of Canada in Montreal on Sunday.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins fourth Formula 1 race in a row after George Russell’s car issues

item.dc:creator: Amina Kilpatrick
Mark Hodge
Kayla Hayempour
item.description: President Donald Trump said he won’t “rush into a deal” to end the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran after senior Republican lawmakers warned it could be a “disastrous mistake.”

media:content.media:text: Iran War
media:content.media:description: A man waves an Iranian flag under a billboard depicting the Strait of Hormuz and President Donald Trump in Tehran on May 6, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Yellow Crime scene tape stretches across the frame in front of police officers.
media:content.media:description: Secret Service police place evidence markers Saturday after an exchange of gunfire near the White House.

media:content.media:text: A promotional image for the newly formed Cockroach Janta Party.
media:content.media:description: A promotional image for the newly formed Cockroach Janta Party.

media:content.media:text: Uganda stepped up measures along the DKC border to combat the Ebola outbreak
media:content.media:description: Ugandan health teams screen travelers for Ebola on Saturday as precautions have been stepped on the border with the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: The Ninja Creami is one of the best ice cream makers, but are there better options? I tested it against KitchenAid, Cuisinart Fast Freeze and more to find out.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Cory Fernandez and Zoe Malin
media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Cory Fernandez and Zoe Malin
media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami on a granite table with the mixing paddle beside it.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: Cuisinart FastFreeze ice cream maker on a table.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: Light green KitchenAid Stand Mixer with ice cream maker attachment installed.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The GreenPan Frost sitting on a table with the lid beside it.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker on a table.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

item.title: I tested the Ninja Creami against five popular competitors, and the results shocked me
